dimensions.rationale: Read-only inspection remained limited to the immutable review anchor, its same-tree executed parent, candidate-bound local artifacts, implementation, tests, and preserved review history. No protected Oracle, Gmail, private repository, production workflow, remote write, publication, or Secret access was performed.

The anchor has exactly one parent, that parent is the receipt subject candidate, both commits and the receipt resolve to tree 3a3296c7e68b8c1ad811e6c8e2b6ca03046f848c, and the anchor contains one exact candidate trailer and one exact receipt-digest trailer. Receipt7 has the supplied SHA-256 and records 15 distinct zero-exit LOCAL_SYNTHETIC_ONLY commands. The focused immutable-anchor and post-review-drift regressions passed: 2 passed, 18 deselected.

The evidence consistently distinguishes local synthetic verification from protected or production execution, requires zero protected/remote counters and NOT_RUN observations, preserves prior mixed and adverse verdicts, and does not claim deployment, publication, Gmail/private-repository behavior, RMD-06, RMD-07, or final AC-033.

PRE_CLOSURE remains the fail-closed state unless complete assurance passes; CLOSED requires receipt7-bound v2 task and aggregate evidence plus the Git receipt anchor. Protected effect, workflow, dependency, test, assurance, status, and package drift after review is rejected, so rollback to the non-closed state remains deterministic.

findings.finding: Exact candidate, tree, request, receipt, reply, model-family, ordered-attempt, and distinct platform-task identities are mechanically represented and validated across both model families.
findings.resolution_ref: The final provenance contract requires eight ordered attempts per family, sixteen distinct task IDs, exact historical artifact hashes and verdicts, a common final anchor/tree/request/receipt, and two distinct final replies.

findings.finding: Local gate claims are supported by a candidate-bound structured receipt containing exact command identities, exit codes, tool versions, output metadata, sanitized output, and digests.
findings.resolution_ref: Receipt7 has SHA-256 820b18a3c45891db7ab05d2ed95fed2593f81546dcb7031ce5fc96d707386b1d and contains exactly 15 unique zero-exit commands; validators reject incomplete, duplicated, stale, leaking, or digest-inconsistent evidence.

findings.finding: Effectful write authorization remains coupled to prospective repository, LFS, object, and release-asset demand before callback execution.
findings.resolution_ref: The retained capacity projection, fail-closed authorization, cumulative reservation behavior, and boundary regressions remain in the reviewed tree.

findings.finding: Post-review integrity covers reviewed effect, production-workflow, evidence-capture, dependency, runtime, test, assurance, status, and package authority surfaces.
findings.resolution_ref: Protected-path Git drift is rejected, and the focused adversarial regression passed for representative protected and intentionally unprotected paths.

findings.finding: Delivery status no longer permits crossed PRE_CLOSURE and CLOSED package/finding states, and historical remediation assertions permit only valid successor state.
findings.resolution_ref: Coupled schema alternatives and negative regressions bind v1.0.4 to PRE_CLOSURE facts and v1.0.5 to CLOSED facts while preserving historical artifacts.

findings.finding: The deterministic status builder and validator require complete assurance and protect closure truth-authority surfaces from post-review mutation.
findings.resolution_ref: Only PASS assurance selects CLOSED; construction and independent validation supply the real repository root, enforce Git-bound integrity, and otherwise retain PRE_CLOSURE.

findings.finding: Stage 6 evidence routing supports the declared v1/v2 transition without allowing unsupported or incorrectly integrated evidence versions.
findings.resolution_ref: PRE_CLOSURE preserves eight v1 task records; CLOSED requires eight receipt-bound v2 task records plus the exact aggregate, command subsets, local-only observations, and zero or NOT_RUN protected counters.

findings.finding: Synchronous replacement of receipt7 and every mutable evidence/provenance receipt binding cannot satisfy CLOSED because the immutable review-anchor trailer pins the original exact digest.
findings.resolution_ref: Anchor d257042ab16347d75c23db145b24f24b0306d56f is an empty same-tree child of executed candidate 65b91c8094481f18026a90b6b7778716df8f708e and pins receipt SHA-256 820b18a3c45891db7ab05d2ed95fed2593f81546dcb7031ce5fc96d707386b1d. The regression first validates a genuine same-tree anchor, then synchronizes every mutable receipt binding and confirms validation fails on trailer digest mismatch.

limitations: This review verifies only repository state, immutable local Git objects, deterministic local artifacts, and local synthetic tests; it does not prove protected Oracle behavior, real Gmail behavior, private-repository behavior, production health, deployment, publication, or remote workflow success.
RMD-06, RMD-07, and final AC-033 remain unexecuted and unproven.
The supplied execution receipt is evidence of the recorded local commands and digests, not independent proof of external platform or production effects.
summary: PASS. The final anchor correctly targets d257042ab16347d75c23db145b24f24b0306d56f, preserves the executed tree, pins the exact receipt7 digest through immutable Git trailers, closes the synchronized replacement path, retains all eight-attempt history, and preserves honest local-only scope. Review progress is 100%; no further review iteration is required for RMD-05 on this anchor.
